parseJsonFile.description:
Parses a file in the JavaScript Object Notation format, converting it to the engine's Serialization format types.

Notes:
- Vectors aren't supported.
- All numeric values will be SerializedFloats, because JSON does not differentiate between number formats.

SerializedValue.description: A basic serialized value, base class for representing a serialized data hierarchy.

toJsonString.description:
Converts this value to a string containing JSON data.
The JSON format does not support vectors. This function will fail if this value contains or is a vector.

arguments.prettyPrint: If false, the JSON will be written in a compact format. If true, it will be indented and each value will be put on a separate line.

toJsonString.return: The string containing the JSON data if the function succeeds. Otherwise it returns null.
